title: Basic TIG and Stick Welding Safety and Setup
summary: This section introduces TIG and Stick welding processes available on the Vulcan OmniPro 220, covering which materials each process is suited for. It also outlines mandatory protective gear requirements and emphasizes the importance of practice welding on scrap material before working on actual projects.
key_facts: DC TIG Welding is used to weld mild steel and stainless steel using a TIG Rod and shielding gas.
AC TIG Welding is used to weld aluminum using a TIG Rod and shielding gas.
Stick Welding is used to weld mild steel and stainless steel using a Stick Electrode without shielding gas.
Good welding takes a degree of skill and experience.
Practice welds are recommended whenever welding a different thickness of material.
Practice welds are recommended whenever welding a different type of material.
Practice welds are recommended whenever welding a different type of connection.
Practice welds are recommended whenever using a different process.
Light from the arc can cause permanent damage to the eyes and skin.
Keep welding tip clear of grounded objects whenever unit is plugged in and turned on.
warnings: TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY: Protective gear must be worn when using the Welder; minimum shade number 10 full face shield (or welding mask), ear protection, welding gloves, sleeves and apron, NIOSH-approved respirator, and fire resistant work clothes without pockets should be worn when welding.
Light from the arc can cause permanent damage to the eyes and skin.
TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY, FIRE AND BURNS: Keep welding tip clear of grounded objects whenever unit is plugged in and turned on.
Read the ENTIRE IMPORTANT SAFETY INFORMATION section at the beginning of this manual including all text under subheadings therein before set up or use of this product.
